voice_ai_rag.label: Voice AI / RAG
voice_ai_rag.audit_hook: We audit voice agent architectures for latency, failover, and prompt injection vulnerabilities.

steps.subject: Your {{project_name}} project — a quick note from Buteforce
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

I came across your Upwork listing for {{project_name}} and noticed the work you did with voice AI / RAG systems at {{company}}.

Building production-grade conversational AI is deceptively hard — most teams hit the same three walls: latency spikes under load, retrieval hallucinations, and failover gaps when the LLM provider throttles.

We're Buteforce, an AI engineering studio that specialises in exactly this. We work with companies that have already shipped v1 and need it to be enterprise-reliable.

Would a 15-minute call make sense? Happy to look at your current architecture first and come with specific observations rather than a sales pitch.

{{sender_name}}
{{sender_title}}, Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
You're receiving this because {{company}} has published AI engineering roles. Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]

steps.subject: One thing most RAG pipelines get wrong (relevant to {{company}})
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

Following up on my last note. Wanted to share something concrete rather than just checking in.

The most common failure we see in production RAG systems: chunk boundaries that break semantic meaning. It kills retrieval precision silently — the system looks fine in demos but degrades badly on real user queries.

We wrote a short internal guide on fixing this without a full reindex. Happy to share it — no strings attached.

Also, if you're exploring extending your {{project_name}} work, we're taking on a couple of new architecture engagements this quarter.

{{sender_name}}
Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]

Interested? Just reply and we'll set it up.

n8n_langchain.label: N8N / LangChain / Automation
n8n_langchain.audit_hook: We audit automation workflows for data leakage and webhook security exposures.

steps.subject: {{project_name}} — Buteforce wanted to say hello
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

I spotted your {{project_name}} project on Upwork and it caught my eye — we work almost exclusively with teams building serious automation and agentic systems.

Most N8N / LangChain builds we see are technically working but architecturally fragile: hardcoded credentials in workflow configs, no circuit breakers on external API calls, and agent loops with no token-budget guardrails.

We're Buteforce — we come in at the architecture layer, not as extra dev hands.

Worth a quick call to compare notes on what you've built?

{{sender_name}}
{{sender_title}}, Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]

steps.subject: N8N webhook security — something worth checking at {{company}}
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

Quick follow-up. One thing we check in every automation audit: are your N8N webhooks exposed without IP allowlisting or HMAC signature verification?

It's a very common gap — and it means anyone who discovers the webhook URL can trigger your workflows. We've seen this lead to runaway AI spend and data exfiltration in production systems.

Happy to do a free 30-minute webhook security review for {{company}} if you'd like a second set of eyes.

{{sender_name}}
Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]

steps.subject: Free Automation Security Audit — {{company}}
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

Final note. We're running a free Architectural AI Audit for automation-heavy teams this quarter. For workflow/agentic builds like {{project_name}}, that means:

- Webhook exposure and authentication review
- Credential handling audit (env vars, secret stores)
- Agent loop guardrails and cost runaway prevention
- Written findings report delivered in 5 business days

No commitment required. If you want it, just reply.

{{sender_name}}
Buteforce | AI Engineering Studio
https://buteforce.com/ai-audit

computer_vision.label: Computer Vision
computer_vision.audit_hook: We review CV pipelines for model drift, edge case failures, and deployment bottlenecks.

steps.subject: {{project_name}} — Buteforce intro
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

I came across your {{project_name}} project and wanted to reach out — computer vision systems in production are one of our specialties at Buteforce.

The hardest part is rarely the initial model accuracy. It's production: model drift as real-world data distribution shifts, edge-case failures your test set never surfaced, and inference latency that works on a GPU but fails on the actual deployment target.

We help teams build CV systems that are architecturally sound from the start — not just accurate in the lab.

Would a short call to discuss {{company}}'s current setup make sense?

{{sender_name}}
{{sender_title}}, Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]

steps.subject: Model drift monitoring — are you tracking it at {{company}}?
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

Following up. The most underestimated risk in production CV systems is silent model drift — the model degrades gradually as real-world inputs shift away from training distribution, and nobody notices until defect/error rates spike.

A proper drift monitoring setup catches this early. We've implemented it for several clients and it's become one of the highest-ROI things we deliver.

If {{company}} isn't tracking prediction confidence distribution over time, it's worth a conversation.

{{sender_name}}
Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]

fullstack_ai.label: Full-Stack AI / General
fullstack_ai.audit_hook: We audit AI codebases for hardcoded keys, insecure endpoints, and scalability ceilings.

steps.subject: {{project_name}} — reaching out from Buteforce
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

I came across your {{project_name}} listing on Upwork and wanted to introduce Buteforce. We're an AI engineering studio — we work with companies that have already shipped AI-powered products and need architecture-level thinking to scale them safely.

The most common gaps we see at this stage: hardcoded API keys in deployed environments, no rate limiting on AI endpoints, and vendor lock-in that becomes painful at scale.

Would it be useful to compare notes on what {{company}} has built and where the gaps might be?

{{sender_name}}
{{sender_title}}, Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]

steps.subject: AI security gaps most teams miss — relevant to {{company}}
steps.body:
Hi {{contact_name}},

Following up with something concrete. The three AI security issues we find most often in full-stack AI products:

1. API keys in frontend bundles or server logs
2. No prompt injection guardrails on user-facing LLM calls
3. Unthrottled AI endpoints that can be abused to run up your OpenAI bill

Any of these ring a bell for {{company}}'s current setup? Happy to talk through mitigations.

{{sender_name}}
Buteforce
https://buteforce.com

---
Unsubscribe: [unsubscribe_link]
